reasoning:
The question asks whether CSGP's close price on three specific dates will be strictly greater than its price on the forecast date (Nov 9, 2025, substituting the Nov 7 Friday close). 

CSGP recently experienced a ~15-18% drop after its Q3 earnings on Oct 28, 2025, due to an unexpected $30.9M net loss and mixed guidance. The stock is also grappling with negative sentiment from a $1B+ lawsuit from Zillow. 

Historically, stocks suffering severe earnings misses and large single-day drops experience negative Post-Earnings Announcement Drift (PEAD) for 30-60 days as institutional adjustment occurs. Furthermore, because CSGP has fallen by ~30% from its 52-week high earlier in 2025, it is highly susceptible to year-end tax-loss harvesting, which creates broad selling pressure through November and December. 

1. For 2025-11-16 (1 week out), negative momentum from the fresh earnings miss and legal woes is strongest, pushing the likelihood of a positive price move slightly below a coin-flip. 
2. For 2025-12-09 (~1 month out), PEAD continues alongside peak tax-loss harvesting season, preventing a strong bullish reversal. 
3. For 2026-02-07 (~3 months out), the negative catalyst will be largely priced in. The standard positive drift of broad equity markets, combined with the "January effect" (where prior-year losers often rebound early in the new year), tilts the probability back into slight bullish territory. 

Per the prompt instructions to increase uncertainty for distant forecasts, these probabilities progressively converge near 0.50 (distances from 0.5 are 0.04, 0.02, and 0.01 respectively).
